methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA creates institutional infrastructure for distributed AI literacy beyond elite tech corridors, potentially seeding peer-to-peer learning networks that could spread deep capability across Richmond's diverse communities rather than concentrating it in university labs.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia signals bottom-up capacity building in a traditionally underserved region, representing exactly the distributed, deep learning model that could counteract metro concentration—if it achieves genuine AI fluency rather than surface-level platform use.

signals.title: AI trainer says workers must lead technology, not follow it - WDBJ7
signals.signal_reading: An AI trainer emphasizing worker agency over passive adoption in Southwest Virginia directly addresses the deep vs. shallow question: this signals potential for Build It Yourself outcomes if workers gain foundational understanding, or risks Widening Gap if training remains theoretical without hands-on implementation infrastructure.

signals.title: Hampton Roads multiple listing service introduces AI tool to personalize home searches - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Hampton Roads MLS deploying turnkey AI search tools exemplifies shallow-but-distributed adoption: real estate agents gain AI capabilities through platforms requiring minimal technical depth, spreading capability geographically while keeping the actual AI knowledge concentrated with vendors.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: Richmond's young workforce feeling AI pressure without clear pathways to capability signals the Widening Gap scenario: awareness and anxiety are distributing faster than actual skills or tools, creating demand without the institutional responses needed for either deep learning or accessible platforms.
